2026-04-23T10:29:14.4754831Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T10:29:14.4755442Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T10:29:14.4755876Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T10:29:14.4756245Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T10:29:14.4756556Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T10:29:14.4756791Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T10:29:14.4756945Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-23T10:29:14.4758238Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T10:30:14.74882193Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58594,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Docs: link new client-options and Get-ResourceHierarchy docs from README and docs index\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** f3b95de (PR #58358) and 1b85421 (PR #57924) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nTwo PRs merged to `main` on 2026-04-23 each introduced new documentation files for the Azure Management Generator emitter package:\\n\\n1. **PR #57924** added `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` — full reference for the `@`@clientOption`` decorator keys recognized by the emitter (`resource-rbac-roles` and the newly added `resource-name-constraint`).\\n2. **PR #58358** added `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1` and its companion `Get-ResourceHierarchy.md` — a reflection-based analyzer that extracts the resource hierarchy from a compiled management-plane DLL and outputs JSON.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` has no mention of `@`@clientOption`` support, no pointer to `docs/client-options.md`, and no mention of the `Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1` utility script\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx` links to `emitter.md` but does not link to the new `client-options.md`, leaving the `@`@clientOption`` reference effectively orphaned\\n- Neither the `resource-name-constraint` nor the `resource-rbac-roles` client options are discoverable from the main README entry point\\n- The `Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1` script and its `.md` exist only in `eng/scripts/` with no cross-reference from any higher-level docs\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx`\\n\\n**Edit** — add a link to `client-options.md` after the existing `[See documentation](./emitter.md)` line:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n\\n## Customizing code generation with `@`@clientOption``\\n\\n[See documentation](./client-options.md)\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — add two new sections after the existing emitter options block:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Customizing code generation with `@`@clientOption``\\n\\nThe emitter supports several [`@`@clientOption``]((azure.github.io/redacted) decorator keys scoped to `\\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;` that allow spec authors to provide additional metadata for resource code generation.\\n\\nSee [Supported @`@clientOption` Keys](./docs/client-options.md) for the full reference, including:\\n\\n- **`resource-rbac-roles`** — defines RBAC role definitions used by the provisioning generator to emit `BuiltInRole` structs\\n- **`resource-name-constraint`** — overrides min/max length and pattern constraints for resource names in generated provisioning code\\n\\n## Utility scripts\\n\\n### `Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1`\\n\\nA reflection-based analyzer that extracts the resource hierarchy from a compiled `Azure.ResourceManager..dll` and outputs the result as JSON. Useful for validating resource hierarchies during management-plane SDK migrations and during design review.\\n\\n```powershell\\npwsh eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1  \\u0026gt; hierarchy.json\\n```\\n\\nSee [Get-ResourceHierarchy documentation](./eng/scripts/Get-ResourceHierarchy.md) for full usage, output schema, and caveats.\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed snippets are needed for this change — the implementation guide refers only to static documentation updates with TypeSpec and PowerShell code blocks that do not require snippet extraction.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `README.md` at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is a developer-tooling README (not a client library README), so the full client library section template does not apply. After the changes in Step 1, the README should at minimum contain:\\n\\n1. **Install** — already present ✅\\n2. **Emitter usage** — already present ✅\\n3. **Emitter options** — already present ✅\\n4. **Customizing code generation with `@`@clientOption``** — **missing**, add per Step 1 above\\n5. **Utility scripts** — **missing**, add per Step 1 above\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` (if applicable — the emitter itself is TypeScript/npm)\\n2. Verify Markdown renders correctly by reviewing the diff for `README.md` and `docs/index.mdx`\\n3. No `Update-Snippets.ps1` or `Export-API.ps1` runs are needed for this change\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24829869934/agentic_workflow) · ● 482.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58594\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-23T10:27:18Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-23T10:27:18Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

additional metadata for resource code generation.\\n\\nSee [Supported @`@clientOption` Keys](./docs/client-options.md) for the full reference, including:\\n\\n- **`resource-rbac-roles`** — defines `Azure.ResourceManager..dll` and outputs the result as JSON. Generator] Docs: link new client-options and Get-ResourceHierarchy docs from README and docs index\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** f3b95de (PR #58358) and 1b85421 (PR #57924) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nTwo PRs merged to `main` on 2026-04-23 each introduced new documentation files for the Azure Management Generator emitter package:\\n\\n1. **PR #57924** added `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` — full reference for the `@`@clientOption`` decorator keys recognized by the emitter (`resource-rbac-roles` and the newly added `resource-name-constraint`).\\n2. **PR #58358** added `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1` and its companion `Get-ResourceHierarchy.md` — a reflection-based analyzer that extracts the resource hierarchy from a compiled management-plane DLL and outputs JSON.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` has no mention of `@`@clientOption`` support, no pointer to `docs/client-options.md`, and no mention of the `Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1` utility script\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx` links to `emitter.md` but does not link to the new `client-options.md`, leaving the `@`@clientOption`` reference effectively orphaned\\n- Neither the `resource-name-constraint` nor the `resource-rbac-roles` client options are discoverable from the main README entry point\\n- The `Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1` script and its `.md` exist only in `eng/scripts/` with no cross-reference from any higher-level docs\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx`\\n\\n**Edit** — add a link to `client-options.md` after the existing `[See documentation](./emitter.md)` line:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n\\n## Customizing code generation with `@`@clientOption``\\n\\n[See documentation](./client-options.md)\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — add two new sections after the existing emitter options block:\\n\\n````markdown\\n## Customizing code generation with `@`@clientOption``\\n\\nThe emitter supports several [`@`@clientOption``]((azure.github.io/redacted) decorator keys scoped to `\\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;` that allow spec authors to provide additional metadata for resource code generation.\\n\\nSee [Supported @`@clientOption` Keys](./docs/client-options.md) for the full reference, including:\\n\\n- **`resource-rbac-roles`** — defines RBAC role definitions used by the provisioning generator to emit `BuiltInRole` structs\\n- **`resource-name-constraint`** — overrides min/max length and pattern constraints for resource names in generated provisioning code\\n\\n## Utility scripts\\n\\n### `Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1`\\n\\nA reflection-based analyzer that extracts the resource hierarchy from a compiled `Azure.ResourceManager..dll` and outputs the result as JSON. Useful for validating resource hierarchies during management-plane SDK migrations and during design review.\\n\\n```powershell\\npwsh eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Get-ResourceHierarchy.ps1  \\u0026gt; hierarchy.json\\n```\\n\\nSee [Get-ResourceHierarchy documentation](./eng/scripts/Get-ResourceHierarchy.md) for full usage, output schema, and caveats.\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed snippets are needed for this change — the implementation guide refers only to static documentation updates with TypeSpec and PowerShell code blocks that do not require snippet extraction.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `README.md` at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is a developer-tooling README (not a client library README), so the full client library section template does not apply. After the changes in Step 1, the README should at minimum contain:\\n\\n1. **Install** — already present ✅\\n2. **Emitter usage** — already present ✅\\n3. **Emitter options** — already present ✅\\n4. **Customizing code generation with `@`@clientOption``** — **missing**, add per Step 1 above\\n5. **Utility scripts** — **missing**, add per Step 1 above\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` (if applicable — the emitter itself is TypeScript/npm)\\n2. Verify Markdown renders correctly by reviewing the diff for `README.md` and `docs/index.mdx`\\n3. No `Update-Snippets.ps1` or `Export-API.ps1` runs are needed for this change\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24829869934/agentic_workflow) · ● 482.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58594\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-23T10:27:18Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-23T10:27:18Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-23T10:33:16.8285080Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T10:30:39.641463813Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":1,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4288781055,\"number\":58330,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Missing Usage/Prerequisites section in http-client-csharp-mgmt README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58323 by `@JoshLove-msft`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58323 added `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts to both `http-client-csharp` and `http-client-csharp-mgmt` packages, and integrated doc regeneration into `Generate.ps1`. As part of this, the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` was auto-regenerated using `tspd doc`. However, the `tspd` tool only regenerates emitter option documentation — it does not preserve or generate the human-authored `## Usage` section.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is missing the `## Usage` section (with `### Prerequisite` and `### Customizing Generated Code` sub-sections) that exists in the parallel `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n- The Prerequisites sub-section (Node.js and .NET SDK version requirements) is absent, leaving mgmt emitter users without setup guidance\\n- There is no link to a Customization Guide for mgmt-specific generated code (comparable to the one in the data-plane README)\\n- Neither README documents the new `regen-docs` and `regen-docs:only` npm scripts — developers modifying emitter options manually need to know they can run `npm run regen-docs` to regenerate the README\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Insert the following `## Usage` section after the `## Install` section and before the `## Emitter usage` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Usage\\n\\n### Prerequisite\\n\\n- Install [Node.js]((nodejs.org/redacted) 20 or above. (Verify by running `node --version`)\\n- Install [**.NET 10.0 SDK**]((dotnet.microsoft.com/redacted) for your specific platform. (or a higher version within the 10.0.\\\\* band)\\n\\n### Customizing Generated Code\\n\\nFor detailed instructions on how to customize the generated C# code for Azure management libraries, see the [Customization Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md).\\n\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```bash\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — Add a \\\"Regenerating Documentation\\\" sub-section under `## Usage`, after `### Customizing Generated Code`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Regenerating Documentation\\n\\nThe emitter option documentation in this README is auto-generated. To regenerate it after making changes to emitter options, run:\\n\\n```bash\\nnpm run regen-docs\\n```\\n\\nThis rebuilds the emitter and then regenerates all option documentation using `tspd`. When running `Generate.ps1`, docs are regenerated automatically via `regen-docs:only` (without rebuilding the emitter).\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNot applicable — these are tool/generator package READMEs, not Azure SDK client library packages. No snippet test files apply.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` should contain these sections after the fix:\\n1. `## Install` ✅ (already present)\\n2. `## Usage` ❌ **missing** — add it\\n   - `### Prerequisite`\\n   - `### Customizing Generated Code`\\n   - `### Regenerating Documentation`\\n3. `## Emitter usage` ✅ (already present)\\n4. `## Emitter options` ✅ (already present, auto-generated)\\n\\nReference: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order from the repository root:\\n\\n1. Verify the README changes look correct manually — no build is needed for pure documentation edits to these generator packages\\n2. Optionally, to confirm `regen-docs` still works after the edit (note: `tspd` only regenerates the `## Emitter options` section, so the `## Usage` section added manually will be preserved):\\n   ```powershell\\n   cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt\\n   npm install\\n   npm run regen-docs\\n   ```\\n   Check that the `## Usage` section is still present in the regenerated file (it should be, since `tspd` only affects the emitter options sections it generates).\\n\\n\\u003c/details\\u003e\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u003e [!TIP]\\n\\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign
